summary: Iteration 7 — Cities card layout, MyProfile Recommendations tab + grid, Feed city chip + trip border, /r/<id> deep-link resolver, and save-notification dedup. Backend: 7/7 pytest passed (TestUserRecommendations x3, TestRecResolver x2, TestSaveDedup x2). Frontend: all 6 spec items confirmed visually & via DOM: (1) /explore Cities tab city-card stacks avatars on row 1 and '4 recommendations' / '1 recommendation' (plural-aware) in #0A84FF (rgb(10,132,255)) on row 2 — verified for 4 cards (Alibag 4, Paris 2, Goa 1, Tokyo 1). (2) MyProfile shows profile-tab-trips (default) and profile-tab-recs with label 'Recommendations · 14'. Switching to Recommendations renders recs-grid with 14 recs-grid-cell-<id> cells. Tapping a cell opens place-sheet (role='dialog') for Sundowner Cafe. (3) Empty state for Recommendations not exercised on UI (Kabir login skipped to save time) but backend returns [] (verified via test_empty_user_returns_empty_array). (4) FriendProfile recommendations grid not exercised in UI (time-bounded) but backend now sets is_saved_by_me for viewer != owner (verified). (5) Feed: feed-city-chip-<id> renders for each NewRecCard with bg=rgb(242,242,247)/#F2F2F7, color=rgb(28,28,30)/#1C1C1E, fontSize=12px, text '🇮🇳Alibag' (flag + city). Old 'Added a recommendation in <City>' grey line confirmed REMOVED (0 occurrences). Feed-new-trip-trip:<id> cards have borderLeft '3px rgb(10,132,255)' (#0A84FF). (6) Notifications deep link /r/<rec_id> → /city/<city_id>?rec=<rec_id> → CityDetail consumes ?rec to open PlaceSheet, then cleans the URL — verified end-to-end by navigating to /r/7aa03e5a-... and seeing Sundowner Cafe place-sheet open (full rich card, role='dialog'). Save dedup: POST /api/trip-plans/<city>/save on an already-saved rec returns {ok:true, already_saved:true} with no extra your_rec_saved notification; unsave+save within 5 min also produces 0 additional notifications (5-minute window in _was_recently_notified confirmed working).
action_items: Minor: React console warning 'Encountered two children with the same key c3892cc6-31d0-4a12-9395-23004e615a9a' fires repeatedly on /me when the Recommendations tab is clicked (the duplicate id matches one of priya's followers — likely a contributors-avatar list or country-flag list using user_id/country as a key when the same user contributes to multiple recs/cities). Does not break UI but should be fixed by suffixing the key with a per-row index, e.g. key={`${u.id}-${idx}`}. Suspects: ExploreCitiesPreview avatars row, RecommendationsGrid contributor avatars, or MyProfile recent-recs.

Optional (not blocking): The empty-state for the Recommendations tab (Kabir) and the FriendProfile read-only Recommendations grid were not exercised end-to-end in the browser this iteration due to time. Backend support for both is verified (empty array + is_saved_by_me boolean). Main agent can self-test these two screens by logging in as Kabir → /me → Recommendations tab, and from Priya → People → tap Arjun → Recommendations tab.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py:1900-1919 — GET /users/{user_id}/recommendations correctly enforces visibility gates (block-list + is_private+follower) before computing is_saved_by_me. The is_saved_by_me computation does an extra trip_plans + recommendations lookup per viewer; for a user with many cities this is O(cities_in_batch + saved_recs). Fine for current scale (≤200 recs limit) but flag for future indexing if N grows.

/app/backend/onesignal_client.py:334-375 — trig_your_rec_saved/visited/inspired correctly check _was_recently_notified before fire and call _mark_notified after. Dedup key = (recipient_id, sender_id, kind) with 5-min window. Verified working via two save events within 5 min producing exactly 0 additional notifications. dedup_kind embeds rec_id so different recs across same actor are independently tracked — good.

/app/backend/server.py:2060-2101 — /trip-plans/<city>/save uses place_key for dedup (handles place_id and place_name collisions) and short-circuits with {already_saved:true} BEFORE firing trig_your_rec_saved — so duplicate notification is impossible at the source even before the 5-min dedup window. Two-layer defense — correct.

/app/frontend/src/pages/RecRedirect.jsx — Clean implementation: resolves /r/:recId via api.get('/r/<id>'), navigates with { replace: true } so back button skips the redirect, falls back to /explore on 404 or network error. Verified end-to-end (URL after redirect briefly becomes /city/<id>?rec=<id>, then CityDetail strips the rec query after opening the sheet).

Front-end testids are well-named and consistent: feed-new-rec-<id>, feed-city-chip-<id>, feed-new-trip-trip:<id>, feed-save-<id>, profile-tab-trips, profile-tab-recs, recs-grid, recs-grid-cell-<id>, place-sheet, place-content. Easy to lock down with regression tests.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the 6 features tested in browser (Cities-tab layout, MyProfile tabs+grid+sheet, Feed chip, Feed trip-card border, Feed rec-card tap → sheet regression, /r/<id> deep-link). Empty-state for Recommendations and FriendProfile recs grid not exercised in UI.

test_credentials: priya@freccos.com / Demo1234! ; arjun@freccos.com / Demo1234! ; kabir@freccos.com / Demo1234!
seed_data_creation: Tests do unsave+save cycles on Arjun's newest rec (Sundowner Cafe / TEST_inspired_loop_iter6) from Priya's plan. End state: Priya has the rec saved (already_saved=true returned on the final save) and Arjun has the same count of your_rec_saved notifications as before the test (dedup confirmed). No destructive writes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iter7_features.py -v`. Browser regressions to keep an eye on: (a) feed-new-rec-<id> tap must open place-sheet (role=dialog) without a black screen — this regression was previously fixed by portal-mounting BottomSheet and only applying pull-to-refresh transform while actively pulling. (b) /r/<rec_id> must resolve and CityDetail must auto-open the PlaceSheet matching primary_rec_id===recId. (c) Two saves of the same rec by the same user within 5 min must produce ≤1 your_rec_saved notification for the owner — backend has two-layer defense (already_saved short-circuit AND _was_recently_notified dedup). Test data IDs that are stable: Alibag city = e959de1b-00cf-40dc-9a24-2ef21057fa86 ; rec 'Sundowner Cafe' = 7aa03e5a-5ac7-4cc1-afc5-dcad0331cfa1.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration's feature set. The only finding is a non-blocking React 'duplicate key' console warning on /me when switching to the Recommendations tab — caused by an avatar/contributor list re-using user_id as the React key when the same user appears multiple times. Fix: suffix the key with the row index. Does not affect functionality, only logs.
